shared_properties: The barracks uses an 80x80px footprint that sits taller and denser than civilian buildings.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Military cues rely on silhouettes such as banners, shields, and a darker doorway rather than color.
differences: This building anchors warrior training and raid-readiness feedback inside the village overview.
Roof spikes and a shield rack differentiate it from the longhouse and warehouse at a glance.
The entry shadow remains deep so the military hall reads as fortified even in bright snow conditions.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Static building sprite representing the village military hall for training, raid-defense, and readiness states.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000013.png during building boot and place it on the barracks plot as a static sprite.
runtime_notes: Keep the taller silhouette clear against the white basin by using a strong roof outline and dark doorway.
Reserve the top corners for banner silhouettes that remain readable in grayscale.
Do not add ambient motion so raid-state character swaps stay the primary movement cue near the barracks.

context.usage: Military hall used to communicate warrior training capacity and raid readiness in the settlement view.

shape.silhouette: Tall square hall with dark doorway, peaked roof, shield rack, and narrow banners.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 80x80px with all roof and banner shapes inside the bounds.
The building reads as more fortified than art_000011 and art_000012 at 1x scale.
Shield rack and doorway remain legible over snow and background cliff tones.
